Do ethnic disparities exist in disease burden and healthcare utilization of male breast cancer? A 9-year cohort study of 9.08 million adults in China

evidence_entities.text: Male breast cancer (MBC) is a rare disease accounting for approximately 1 % of all breast cancer
Mongolian had a higher age-standardized 5-year prevalence rate than Han (3.2[95% CI: 2.5–4.0] vs 2.3[95% CI: 1.7–3.0], P=0.016)
no significant differences existed in incidence rates, survival rates, and risk of all-cause and breast cancer-specific mortality
few significant disparities in healthcare utilization between Han and Mongolian were observed
A retrospective cohort study was established during 2012–2021 based on the Inner Mongolia Regional Health Information Platform
BRCA2 mutations were more common in MBC
